title: Open the application's starting page in a fresh browser
goal: Confirm that someone with no account can reach a meaningful starting page that explains what to do next.
preconditions: The application is installed and reachable at its expected URL.
No accounts have been created yet.
You are using a browser with no prior cookies for this application (a new private/incognito window works).

steps.action: Open a fresh private/incognito browser window. Navigate to the application URL.
steps.expected: A page loads. The page makes it clear what kind of application this is and what someone arriving for the first time should do next. There is a visible way to begin setup or create the first account.

steps.action: Read the page. Look for any text directed at a brand-new user.
steps.expected: You can identify, within 30 seconds of looking, what the next step is.

expected_overall: A first-time visitor with no account has a clear path forward and is not stuck staring at a sign-in form for an account that doesn't exist.
pass_criteria: Within 30 seconds of the page loading, you knew what to do next.

title: Register the first administrator account
goal: Create the initial administrator user that will configure the rest of the system.

steps.action: From the starting page, choose the action that begins setup or creates the first account.
steps.expected: A registration form appears with fields for at least name, email, and password (or equivalent identity setup).

steps.action: Fill in plausible administrator details and submit.
steps.expected: The account is created. You are signed in. The next step in setting up the company is clear.

expected_overall: An administrator account exists and is signed in, ready to configure the tenant.
pass_criteria: Account creation completed without errors AND you are signed in as that account.

title: Set the company identity
goal: Configure the company's name, address, and primary contact information.
preconditions: You are signed in as the first administrator.

steps.action: Find and open the company settings or organization profile area.
steps.expected: A settings page appears with editable fields for company name, address, phone, etc.

steps.action: Enter a company name, address, and phone number. Save the settings.
steps.expected: A confirmation appears. The values you entered are visible after the save and persist across reload.

expected_overall: The company identity is saved and visible in the application's chrome (header, page titles, etc.) where appropriate.
pass_criteria: Saved values match what was entered AND survived a page reload.

title: Create the first work center
goal: Configure a physical or logical area where production work happens. Work orders will be assigned to work centers.
preconditions: You are signed in as a user with permission to configure work centers.

steps.action: Find and open the work centers area.
steps.expected: A list of existing work centers appears. The list is empty (or shows only seed data clearly marked as such).

steps.action: Choose the action to create a new work center. Fill in a name (e.g. "Press Shop"), a location, and a default labor rate.
steps.expected: The form accepts the values. Save succeeds.

expected_overall: The new work center is visible in the list and can be selected when creating routings later.
pass_criteria: Work center exists in the list AND opens to show the values you entered.

title: Create the first vendor
goal: Add a supplier so that purchase orders can later be issued against them.
preconditions: You are signed in as a user with permission to manage vendors.

steps.action: Find and open the vendors area.
steps.expected: A list of vendors appears, empty by default.

steps.action: Choose the action to create a new vendor. Enter vendor name, contact, address, and payment terms.
steps.expected: The form accepts the values. Save succeeds.

expected_overall: Vendor is created and visible in the list.
pass_criteria: Vendor record exists with the entered values.

title: Start work on an assigned work order
goal: Begin work on an open work order from the floor.
preconditions: An open work order is assigned to your work center.
You have a scanner or input device available.
You are signed in to the floor application.

steps.action: Scan or select the work order ID. Then scan or select the start action.
steps.expected: The work order changes to in-progress status. The current operation, target quantity, and any setup instructions are visible.
The work center is shown and matches your assigned area.

expected_overall: The work order is in-progress and labor time is being tracked against it.
pass_criteria: Status reflects in-progress AND your user is recorded as the operator AND time tracking has begun.

title: Report damage on a piece of equipment
goal: Log a damage report against a fixed asset, creating a maintenance ticket.
preconditions: A fixed asset record exists for the equipment.
You have a way to identify the asset (asset tag, scanner).

steps.action: Find or scan the asset's record.
steps.expected: The asset's profile opens, showing recent activity and any open tickets.

steps.action: Choose the action to report damage or open a maintenance ticket. Fill in a description and severity.
steps.expected: A ticket is created. It appears in the maintenance team's queue.

expected_overall: Damage is logged. A maintenance ticket exists, linked to the asset, with the description you provided.
pass_criteria: Ticket exists AND links to the correct asset AND appears in the maintenance queue.
